overview: The Marathon County Board of Supervisors held an adjourned organizational meeting where they approved committee appointments, elected Supervisors Hapa and Arstead to the Executive Committee at-large positions, and unanimously approved a resolution to reduce landfill tipping fees for disaster debris following severe weather that impacted the Town of Ringle. The fee reduction, from the standard $65/ton to approximately $36.94/ton, applies to certified storm-related debris through June 17th.

agenda.item: Pledge of Allegiance and moment of reflection
Committee appointments presentation by Chair Gibbs
Election of at-large Executive Committee members
Ballot results announcement for Executive Committee
Resolution 24-26: Out of home placement reserve increase
Resolution 26-26: Landfill tipping fee modification for disaster relief

discussions.body: Chair Gibbs presented committee appointments developed with Vice Chair Boots, emphasizing goals of increasing perspective diversity, developing supervisor leadership, and accommodating preferences. Gibbs noted 34% of the board is in their first or second term. Motion by Supervisor Cavelli, seconded by Supervisor Crims. The motion carried but was not unanimous.

discussions.item: Election of at-large Executive Committee members
discussions.body: Three supervisors were nominated: Supervisor Hapa (nominated by Supervisor Mask), Supervisor Leur (nominated by Supervisor Rosenberg), and Supervisor Arstead (nominated by Supervisor Boots). Supervisor Rosenberg explained her nomination of Leur, stating she had 'seen Supervisor Leur advocate for our county's smallest children and our oldest elders.' Paper ballots were distributed and counted. Supervisors Hapa and Arstead were elected to the Executive Committee.

discussions.item: Resolution 24-26: Out of home placement reserve increase
discussions.body: Motion by Supervisor Morash, seconded by Supervisor Robinson. The resolution was approved from the Health and Human Services Committee and Human Resources and Finance and Property Committee. The motion carried but was not unanimous.
Motion by Supervisor P, seconded by Supervisor Robinson. The resolution to amend the 2026 social services budget was approved. The motion carried but was not unanimous.

discussions.item: Resolution 26-26: Landfill tipping fee modification for disaster relief
discussions.body: Administrator Leonard and Director Hagen Busher presented the proposal following severe weather that damaged approximately 44 homes in Ringle. The solid waste department processed about 400 tons of emergency debris. The resolution reduces tipping fees from the standard $65/ton to approximately $36.94/ton for certified disaster debris, with the state waiving $13.10 and Ringle waiving $2.33. Director Hagen Busher noted Brown County and Dane County only dropped the state fee, and warned that Dane County lost FEMA reimbursement in the 1990s after fully waiving fees. The board suspended rules (unanimous) to consider the resolution directly, then approved it unanimously. Motion by Supervisor Arstead, seconded by Supervisor Morash.

publicComment: Special dispensation was granted for two residents to speak on the landfill tipping fee resolution. Jessica Tolesie spoke first, requesting the county wave all fees for six months, noting the state, DNR, and Ringle are waiving their portions, and that private businesses have donated up to $40,000 in hauling services. She stated Marathon County had not provided significant financial assistance. Matt Haybeck spoke second, requesting fee waivers for the approximately 44 homeowners facing demolition, noting some insurance adjusters have not yet arrived and residents need more time.
actionItems: Committee appointments approved as presented by Chair Gibbs and Vice Chair Boots
Supervisors Hapa and Arstead elected to Executive Committee at-large positions
Resolution 24-26 approved for out of home placement reserve increase
Resolution 25-26 approved for social services budget amendment
Resolution 26-26 approved reducing landfill tipping fees to approximately $36.94/ton for certified disaster debris through June 17th
Administrator Leonard to work with Community Foundation on mechanism for reimbursing unreimbursed insurance costs for landowners
Solid waste management board directed to develop clear policy on handling disaster situations within six months
Staff to track disaster debris costs and report back at May board meeting on potential timeline extension
title: Marathon County Regular Meeting
